note: Paragraphs Edit exposes per-paragraph edit/clone/delete routes+forms, contextual links, revision-aware saving, and two lineage services. It defines no config, no settings, and no permissions of its own (access = core entity access). Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) probing documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site with Paragraphs Edit, I want to edit ONE paragraph directly without opening the whole host node's edit form. What route/path and form does it use?
evals.expected_output: Use the paragraphs_edit.edit_form route, path /paragraphs_edit/{root_parent_type}/{root_parent}/paragraphs/{paragraph}/edit, which renders ParagraphEditForm (paragraph form op entity_edit) showing just that paragraph's fields.

evals.prompt: Which module adds Edit / Clone / Delete contextual links directly on rendered paragraphs, and what core module does that rely on?
evals.expected_output: Paragraphs Edit attaches a `paragraph` contextual-links group (Edit paragraph, Clone paragraph, Delete paragraph) to rendered paragraphs, relying on core's Contextual (contextual) module; links are suppressed on admin/QuickEdit routes.

evals.prompt: When I edit an individual paragraph with Paragraphs Edit on a node type that creates new revisions by default, what happens to revisions? Which service handles it?
evals.expected_output: It is revision-aware: ParagraphLineageRevisioner (paragraphs_edit.lineage.revisioner) checks shouldCreateNewRevision() and, if true, saves the paragraph plus every ancestor as new revisions, updating each parent field item's target_revision_id.

evals.prompt: With Paragraphs Edit, can I clone a paragraph to a different parent entity/field, and which route/form does that? What entity access is required?
evals.expected_output: Yes — paragraphs_edit.clone_form (ParagraphCloneForm, op entity_clone) duplicates the paragraph and lets you choose destination Type/Bundle/Parent/Field; route access requires paragraph.update, and submit validates update access on the destination entity and edit access on the destination field.

evals.prompt: What permission does Paragraphs Edit define to gate editing or deleting a paragraph, and how is access actually enforced on its routes?
evals.expected_output: It defines no permissions of its own; access is core entity access — edit and delete routes require _entity_access root_parent.update (update access to the host entity), clone requires paragraph.update.

evals.prompt: What are the module dependencies of Paragraphs Edit that I must enable for it to work?
evals.expected_output: It depends on paragraphs:paragraphs and drupal:contextual (info.yml); Paragraphs in turn provides entity_reference_revisions.

evals.prompt: In custom Drupal code, how do I find the top-level host entity of a deeply nested paragraph using Paragraphs Edit? Name the service and method.
evals.expected_output: Use the paragraphs_edit.lineage.inspector service (ParagraphLineageInspector) and call getRootParent($paragraph), which walks getParentEntity() up until a non-paragraph host entity is reached.
